name: Cruze, Grant
description: There has been much discussion as to why he came back for his C. E., but only one conclusion has been drawn.
text: There has been much discussion as to why he came back for his C. E., but only one conclusion has been drawn.

name: Adams, Noah
motto: "A youth to fortune and to fame unknown."
description: Until he was suddenly elected to the presidency of the class to fill Dowdle's unexpired term.
text:
"A youth to fortune and to fame unknown."
Until he was suddenly elected to the presidency of the class to fill Dowdle's unexpired term.

name: Boyd, Frances Leone
motto: "Poetry is the blossom and fragrance of all human knowledge, human thoughts, passions, emotions."
description: And Frances' college career prophesies well for her future as a poet. Many of her poems have excited a great deal of wondering comment and praise.
text:
"Poetry is the blossom and fragrance of all human knowledge, human thoughts, passions, emotions."
And Frances' college career prophesies well for her future as a poet. Many of her poems have excited a great deal of wondering comment and praise.

name: Brennan, Mildred
motto: "Modest and shy as a nun is she."
description: She is known chiefly for her talented voice and engaging ways.
text:
"Modest and shy as a nun is she."
She is known chiefly for her talented voice and engaging ways.

name: Bird, Nell
motto: "'Tis beauty that doth oft make women proud, 'Tis virtue that doth make them most admired."
description: And "Nell" has all of these. She has been a leader in college, the orator of her class in her Junior year, and this year Editor-in-Chief of the Arkansan.
text:
"'Tis beauty that doth oft make women proud, 'Tis virtue that doth make them most admired."
And "Nell" has all of these. She has been a leader in college, the orator of her class in her Junior year, and this year Editor-in-Chief of the Arkansan.

name: Armitage, Marguerite
motto: "And cloudy the day, or stormy the night, The sky of her heart was always bright."
description: "Pete" has found that cheerfulness and eternal good spirits are excellent things in woman—and in "Rita" in particular.
text:
"And cloudy the day, or stormy the night, The sky of her heart was always bright."
"Pete" has found that cheerfulness and eternal good spirits are excellent things in woman—and in "Rita" in particular.

name: Blackshire, Deane
motto: "In many ways does the full heart reveal The presence of the love it would conceal!"
description: If it had not been for Deane, the Sapphic never would have been.
text:
"In many ways does the full heart reveal The presence of the love it would conceal!"
If it had not been for Deane, the Sapphic never would have been.

name: Blackmun, Ora
motto: “Her heart and hand both open and both free; For what she has she gives, what thinks, she shows.”
description: She has made more E's than any other member of the class.
text:
“Her heart and hand both open and both free; For what she has she gives, what thinks, she shows.”
She has made more E's than any other member of the class.

name: Wohra, Har Das
motto: “Unobtrusive and quiet, as shy as a maid Is desired to be, and of girls much afraid.”
description: He has won the name of eating degrees.
text:
“Unobtrusive and quiet, as shy as a maid Is desired to be, and of girls much afraid.”
He has won the name of eating degrees.

name: Carroll, H. A. D.
motto: “There is nothing ill can dwell in such a temple; If the ill spirit have so fair a house, Good things will strive to dwell with it.”
description: The boy that was lost in Carroll Cave.
text:
“There is nothing ill can dwell in such a temple; If the ill spirit have so fair a house, Good things will strive to dwell with it.”
The boy that was lost in Carroll Cave.

name: Dyer, C. L.
motto: “I am not merry; but I do beguile The things I am by seeming otherwise.”
description: He is a great lover of music.
text:
“I am not merry; but I do beguile The things I am by seeming otherwise.”
He is a great lover of music.

name: Dunn, H. W.
motto: "The paper dealer who has for his advertisement: 'If you want to get stung buy from me. If you want to get stung worse buy elsewhere.'"
text: "The paper dealer who has for his advertisement: 'If you want to get stung buy from me. If you want to get stung worse buy elsewhere.'"

name: Davenport, Bessie
motto: "Work, for the night is coming, Work, in the glowing sun, Work, while the dew is sparkling—" And then work some more.
description: Bess believes that rest comes only with old age.
text:
"Work, for the night is coming, Work, in the glowing sun, Work, while the dew is sparkling—" And then work some more.
Bess believes that rest comes only with old age.

name: Baker, M. S.
motto: "I tell thee, love is Nature's second sun, / Causing a spring of virtues where he shines."
description: Mabin's personality has been so wrapped up in that of another that it has distinguished itself for little else in the U. of A.
text:
"I tell thee, love is Nature's second sun, / Causing a spring of virtues where he shines."
Mabin's personality has been so wrapped up in that of another that it has distinguished itself for little else in the U. of A.

name: Berry, Margaret
motto: "Her sensibilities are so acute / The fear of being silent makes her mute."
description: Margaret sometimes talks, but it is in German or about it.
text:
"Her sensibilities are so acute / The fear of being silent makes her mute."
Margaret sometimes talks, but it is in German or about it.

name: Hollobaugh, Essie
motto: "If you want knowledge you must toil for it; and if pleasure, you must toil for it. Toil is the law."
description: If Essie ever fails to do anything it will not be through low aim or lack of diligence.
text:
"If you want knowledge you must toil for it; and if pleasure, you must toil for it. Toil is the law."
If Essie ever fails to do anything it will not be through low aim or lack of diligence.

name: Holt, M. L.
motto: "His sparkling surface scarce betrays a thoughtful tide beneath it rolled."
text: "His sparkling surface scarce betrays a thoughtful tide beneath it rolled."

name: Hopper, I. C.
motto: "That which ordinary men are fit for, I am qualified in; and the best of me is diligence."
description: He is best known for his oratorical abilities and for the ease with which he learns.
text:
"That which ordinary men are fit for, I am qualified in; and the best of me is diligence."
He is best known for his oratorical abilities and for the ease with which he learns.

name: English, E. H.
motto: "Can knowledge have no bound but must advance so far to make us wise for ignorance?"
description: If he succeeds as well in his profession as he has on the track, he will make a successful man.
text:
"Can knowledge have no bound but must advance so far to make us wise for ignorance?"
If he succeeds as well in his profession as he has on the track, he will make a successful man.

name: Estes, G. D.
motto: "He slew our dragon, not, so seemed it, knew he had done more than any simplest man might do."
clubs: President, the Y. M. C. A.
description: "Good Old Dan" is the man who put Alpena Pass on the map. Known chiefly as a football star, he is also President of the Y. M. C. A., and has been an all-around man all through school.
text:
"He slew our dragon, not, so seemed it, knew he had done more than any simplest man might do."
"Good Old Dan" is the man who put Alpena Pass on the map. Known chiefly as a football star, he is also President of the Y. M. C. A., and has been an all-around man all through school.

name: Erwin, J. T.
motto: "Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"
description: "Chili" is making a record on the baseball diamond.
text:
"Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"
"Chili" is making a record on the baseball diamond.

name: Keith, A. A.
motto: "Every man has his faults and honesty is his."
description: By his quiet and unassuming ways, Allen has gained many friends and lost none.
text:
"Every man has his faults and honesty is his."
By his quiet and unassuming ways, Allen has gained many friends and lost none.

name: Humphreys, F. A.
motto: "Too young for love? Ah, say not so! Too young? Too young? Ah no! No! No!"
description: "Pats," like other men, his classmates, has been conspicuous for nothing until his Senior year—and then only for his meteor-like interest in a girl!
text:
"Too young for love? Ah, say not so! Too young? Too young? Ah no! No! No!"
"Pats," like other men, his classmates, has been conspicuous for nothing until his Senior year—and then only for his meteor-like interest in a girl!

name: Laser, Lucile
motto: "She was so charitable and so piteous She would weep, if that she saw a mouse."
description: As the "Widow of the Deceased" in the famous trial of the State vs. Dexter S. Bush, Lucile proved herself a worthy member of the Law Club.
text:
"She was so charitable and so piteous She would weep, if that she saw a mouse."
As the "Widow of the Deceased" in the famous trial of the State vs. Dexter S. Bush, Lucile proved herself a worthy member of the Law Club.

name: Winfrey, J. S.
motto: "Yond Cyrus has a mild and gentle look, He dreams too much."
description: But in spite of his dreaming, the Arkansan and the library couldn't exist without him.
text:
"Yond Cyrus has a mild and gentle look, He dreams too much."
But in spite of his dreaming, the Arkansan and the library couldn't exist without him.

name: Mixon, H. D.
motto: 'Wisdom he has, and to his wisdom courage; Temper to that, and unto all success.'
description: Harvey has distinguished himself in everything he has ever undertaken; he is now Business Manager of the Arkansan.
text:
'Wisdom he has, and to his wisdom courage; Temper to that, and unto all success.'
Harvey has distinguished himself in everything he has ever undertaken; he is now Business Manager of the Arkansan.

name: Moorehead, Louise
motto: 'Great feelings hath she of her own, Which lesser souls may never know.'
description: With the spirit to do anything that she thought right and prudent, Louise has met everything in college with fearlessness. She has made herself known as a good public speaker on several occasions.
text:
'Great feelings hath she of her own, Which lesser souls may never know.'
With the spirit to do anything that she thought right and prudent, Louise has met everything in college with fearlessness. She has made herself known as a good public speaker on several occasions.

name: McCarty, R. O.
motto: 'Flattery is the food of fools; Yet now and then your men of wit Will condescend to take a bit.'
description: 'Mac' is one of those changeable persons that you can't tell anything about.
text:
'Flattery is the food of fools; Yet now and then your men of wit Will condescend to take a bit.'
'Mac' is one of those changeable persons that you can't tell anything about.

name: Metcalf, R. J.
motto: "Wise to resolve, and patient to perform."
description: The only Geology major in the class.
text:
"Wise to resolve, and patient to perform."
The only Geology major in the class.

name: Quick, W. C.
motto: "Recognizes ever and anon the breeze of nature stirring in his soul."
description: President of the Agri Club.
text:
"Recognizes ever and anon the breeze of nature stirring in his soul."
President of the Agri Club.

name: Shelton, Wilma
motto: "What she wills to do or say is wisest, virtuousest, discreetest, best."
description: Her sweet disposition and high scholarship have won much admiration during her brief stay here.
text:
"What she wills to do or say is wisest, virtuousest, discreetest, best."
Her sweet disposition and high scholarship have won much admiration during her brief stay here.

name: Shuffield, N. E.
motto: "He is the half part of a blessed man Left to be finished by such as she."
description: He has gained his reputation through his wife.
text:
"He is the half part of a blessed man Left to be finished by such as she."
He has gained his reputation through his wife.

name: Schoolfield, Eunice
motto: "Her air, her manners, all who see admire; Courteous, though coy, and gentle, though retired."
description: Eunice cried because she didn't make E+ in Shakespeare. She is equally good in everything else, too.
text:
"Her air, her manners, all who see admire; Courteous, though coy, and gentle, though retired."
Eunice cried because she didn't make E+ in Shakespeare. She is equally good in everything else, too.

name: Stewart, L. G.
motto: "We have left undone those things we should have done, and done those things we should not have done."
description: The University has seen little of him—as a student!
text:
"We have left undone those things we should have done, and done those things we should not have done."
The University has seen little of him—as a student!

name: Waters, R. F.
motto: "By this face, This seeming brow of justice, did he win The hearts of all he did angle for."
description: He is by no means a thief, yet he manages to get control of most of the money that comes to the boys' dormitory.
text:
"By this face, This seeming brow of justice, did he win The hearts of all he did angle for."
He is by no means a thief, yet he manages to get control of most of the money that comes to the boys' dormitory.

name: Alexander, Reba
motto: "A big, gracious heart—the verdict of all who know her."
text: "A big, gracious heart—the verdict of all who know her."

name: Autrey, J. L.
motto: "Only one better grafter in the University and he's my partner."
text: "Only one better grafter in the University and he's my partner."

name: Batten, J. T.
motto: "The law is a sort of hocus-pocus science that smiles in your face while it picks your pocket."
text: "The law is a sort of hocus-pocus science that smiles in your face while it picks your pocket."

name: Bell, Susan
motto: "To know, to esteem, to love."
text: "To know, to esteem, to love."

name: Bethel, Claude
motto: "God made the country and men made the town."
text: "God made the country and men made the town."

name: Bell, J. E.
motto: "Unforced with punishment, unawed by fear, His words were simple, and his soul sincere."
text: "Unforced with punishment, unawed by fear, His words were simple, and his soul sincere."

name: Blackshare, J. O.
motto: "He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one, Exceeding wise, fair spoken and persuading."
text: "He was a scholar, and a ripe and good one, Exceeding wise, fair spoken and persuading."

name: Bonner, E. C.
motto: "Hath Somnus brushed thy eyelids with his rod?"
text: "Hath Somnus brushed thy eyelids with his rod?"

name: Browne, L. W.
motto: "He that hath a beard is more than a youth."
text: "He that hath a beard is more than a youth."

name: Cammack, G. S.
motto: "There's really nothing else to do, fellows, so let's study."
text: "There's really nothing else to do, fellows, so let's study."

name: Cates, A. W.
motto: "Can any man have a higher notion of the rule of right and the eternal fitness of things?"
text: "Can any man have a higher notion of the rule of right and the eternal fitness of things?"

name: Constant, Mabel
motto: "All eyes you draw, and with the eyes the heart."
text: "All eyes you draw, and with the eyes the heart."

name: Davidson, E. C.
motto: "The eternal feminine doth draw on us."
text: "The eternal feminine doth draw on us."

name: Davis, Lucile
motto: "An honest eye and a pleasant smile. What more could one desire?"
text: "An honest eye and a pleasant smile. What more could one desire?"

name: Duncan, E. E.
motto: "Tho' modest, on his unembarrassed brow Nature hath written, 'Gentleman.'"
text: "Tho' modest, on his unembarrassed brow Nature hath written, 'Gentleman.'"

name: Dunn, J. H.
motto: "That man who hath a tongue is no man If with his tongue he cannot win a woman."
text: "That man who hath a tongue is no man If with his tongue he cannot win a woman."

name: Ellis, R. A.
motto: "Some smack of age in you, some relish of the saltness of time."
text: "Some smack of age in you, some relish of the saltness of time."

name: Forrest, L. S.
motto: "If she undervalue me What care I how fair she be."
text: "If she undervalue me What care I how fair she be."

name: Forwood, Eleanor
motto: "And witty to talk with And pleasant too, to think on."
text: "And witty to talk with And pleasant too, to think on."

name: Garrett, Claude
motto: "Claude has social service aspirations."
text: "Claude has social service aspirations."

name: Gibson, Ruth
motto: "Young in limbs, in judgment old."
text: "Young in limbs, in judgment old."

name: Gilliam, S. E.
motto: "Common sense is the most uncommon thing in existence."
text: "Common sense is the most uncommon thing in existence."

name: Gladson, Marion
motto: "In books, or work or healthful play."
text: "In books, or work or healthful play."

name: Gregg, R. C.
motto: "A man who's not afraid to say his say, Though a whole town's against him."
text: "A man who's not afraid to say his say, Though a whole town's against him."

name: Gregg, Pansy
motto: "Rich in saving common sense."
text: "Rich in saving common sense."

name: Greig, J. K.
motto: "Fond of learning, yet fonder of woman."
text: "Fond of learning, yet fonder of woman."

name: Hale, John J.
motto: "His line is the finest ever. He is a Junior by request."
text: "His line is the finest ever. He is a Junior by request."

name: Hall, W. L.
motto: "I was a goer in my day."
text: "I was a goer in my day."

name: Harding, R. C.
motto: "And the whining schoolboy with his satchel And shining morning face, creeping like a snail Unwillingly to school."
text: "And the whining schoolboy with his satchel And shining morning face, creeping like a snail Unwillingly to school."

name: Harville, A. W.
motto: "Hath thy toil o'er books consumed the midnight oil?"
text: "Hath thy toil o'er books consumed the midnight oil?"

name: Hopper, D. C.
motto: "He could on either side dispute, Confute, change hands and still confute."
text: "He could on either side dispute, Confute, change hands and still confute."

name: Huber, C. A.
motto: "Mankind have a great aversion to intellectual labor."
text: "Mankind have a great aversion to intellectual labor."

name: Hughes, Anna
motto: "It's guid to be merry and wise."
text: "It's guid to be merry and wise."

name: Hughes, Jewel
motto: "True genius, but true woman."
text: "True genius, but true woman."

name: Hurlock, Leslie
motto: "Some are born great, some achieve greatness and some have greatness thrust upon them."
text: "Some are born great, some achieve greatness and some have greatness thrust upon them."

name: Jones, M. F.
motto: "Trust in all things high comes easy to him."
text: "Trust in all things high comes easy to him."

name: Jordan, Pauline
motto: "Of fancy, reason, virtue naught can we bereave."
text: "Of fancy, reason, virtue naught can we bereave."

name: Joyner, J. E.
motto: "Remove not the ancient landmarks."
text: "Remove not the ancient landmarks."

name: Kennard, R. P.
motto: “A vast and imperious mind and a heart large as the sands upon the sea shore.”
text: “A vast and imperious mind and a heart large as the sands upon the sea shore.”

name: Kilgore, Vesta
motto: “Begone, dull care. Thou and I shall never agree.”
text: “Begone, dull care. Thou and I shall never agree.”

name: Knerr, Irene
motto: “Thou would’st be great, Art not without ambition, but without the illness that attend it.”
text: “Thou would’st be great, Art not without ambition, but without the illness that attend it.”

name: Knoch, E. A.
motto: “Exhausting thought, And hiving wisdom with each studious year.”
text: “Exhausting thought, And hiving wisdom with each studious year.”

name: Lake, J. P.
motto: “One vast, substantial smile, Jabe is a royal fellow and popular.”
text: “One vast, substantial smile, Jabe is a royal fellow and popular.”

name: Lee, A. W.
motto: “And still they gazed and still the wonder grew That one small head could carry all he knew.”
text: “And still they gazed and still the wonder grew That one small head could carry all he knew.”

name: Lee, R. D.
motto: “R. D. Lee, sir, of Hendrix College, sir; yes, sir.”
text: “R. D. Lee, sir, of Hendrix College, sir; yes, sir.”

name: Mccain, M. G.
motto: “Women and music should never be dated.”
text: “Women and music should never be dated.”

name: Mcfarlane, Margaurite
motto: “She doeth little kindness Which most leave undone or despise.”
text: “She doeth little kindness Which most leave undone or despise.”

name: Mcfarlane, W. D.
motto: “A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays and confident tomorrows.”
text: “A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays and confident tomorrows.”

name: McGill, W. G.
motto: "He was a verray perfight gentil Knight."
text: "He was a verray perfight gentil Knight."

name: Mckinney, Ruth
motto: "Fair tresses man's imperial race ensnare, And beauty draws us with a single hair."
text: "Fair tresses man's imperial race ensnare, And beauty draws us with a single hair."

name: Moore, Lila
motto: "Of manners gentle, of affections mild."
text: "Of manners gentle, of affections mild."

name: Newton, N. K.
motto: "God said, 'Let Newton be,' and all was right."
text: "God said, 'Let Newton be,' and all was right."

name: Nelson, E. H.
motto: "His fame was great in all the land."
text: "His fame was great in all the land."

name: Owens, Marion
motto: "Looks wise, but you can't always sometimes tell."
text: "Looks wise, but you can't always sometimes tell."

name: Park, May
motto: "Doing good is the only certainly happy action."
text: "Doing good is the only certainly happy action."

name: Parsons, L. C.
motto: "Waited on the government with a claim to wear Sabers by the bucketful, rifles by the pair."
text: "Waited on the government with a claim to wear Sabers by the bucketful, rifles by the pair."

name: Pettigrew, Helen
motto: "A happy life is what the Muses love."
text: "A happy life is what the Muses love."

name: Philips, Bessie
motto: "For such a mind is contemplative and kind."
text: "For such a mind is contemplative and kind."

name: Porter, Florence
motto: "Good sense, which is only the gift of Heaven."
text: "Good sense, which is only the gift of Heaven."

name: Pratt, Joy
motto: "Her deep blue eyes smile constantly."
text: "Her deep blue eyes smile constantly."

name: Redus, Frank
motto: "Slumber is more sweet than toil."
text: "Slumber is more sweet than toil."

name: Robinson, Evalyn
motto: "I thus neglecting worldly ends, all dedicated To closeness and bettering of my mind."
text: "I thus neglecting worldly ends, all dedicated To closeness and bettering of my mind."

name: Scott, Ellen
motto: "Almost but not quite."
text: "Almost but not quite."

name: Smith, E. T.
motto: "Who can tell for what high cause This darling of the gods was born."
text: "Who can tell for what high cause This darling of the gods was born."

name: Stevenson, E. U.
motto: "The bed has been a place of luxury to me, I would not exchange it for all the thrones in the world."
text: "The bed has been a place of luxury to me, I would not exchange it for all the thrones in the world."

name: Stewart, R.
motto: "A moral, sensible, and well-bred man."
text: "A moral, sensible, and well-bred man."

name: Stone, Marion
motto: "From grave to gay, from levity to severe."
text: "From grave to gay, from levity to severe."

name: Stuckey, Helen
motto: "Happy am I; from care I'm free."
text: "Happy am I; from care I'm free."

name: Thompson, L. E.
motto: "He was a man. Take him for all in all I shall not look on his like again."
text: "He was a man. Take him for all in all I shall not look on his like again."

name: Turner, A. S.
motto: "A faultless body and a blameless mind."
text: "A faultless body and a blameless mind."

name: Walls, Louise
motto: "The daintiest last to make the end most sweet."
text: "The daintiest last to make the end most sweet."

name: Williams, B. R.
motto: "I may justly say with the hook-nosed fellow of Rome, 'I came, I saw and overcame.'"
text: "I may justly say with the hook-nosed fellow of Rome, 'I came, I saw and overcame.'"

name: Watson, Damon
motto: "A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance."
text: "A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance."

name: Wiggins, S. B.
motto: "Towering in the confidence of twenty-one."
text: "Towering in the confidence of twenty-one."

name: Wilson, J. F.
motto: "Really a handsome and charming man."
text: "Really a handsome and charming man."

name: Wommack, Harry
motto: "Let me wind my watch—then I'll have time."
text: "Let me wind my watch—then I'll have time."
